volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Casket Comedy (Section 4.1.670 - Section 4.2.746c)

sections.label: Section 4.1.670
sections.original: dedita opera , in tempore ipso ?
sections.translation: for a given purpose , at the very instant

sections.label: Section 4.1.670b
sections.original: Spes mihi sancta subveni .
sections.translation: sacred Hope comes to my aid ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.671
sections.original: Nisi quíd mi opis di dant , dísperii , neque unde aúxilium expetam hábeo .
sections.translation: Unless the Gods give me some aid , I ’ m utterly undone ; nor do I know whence I am to seek for aid . To such a degree does carelessness possess wretched me in mind , which I sadly fear may be lighting upon my own back , if my mistress knows that I ’ m so negligent as I really am .

sections.label: Section 4.2.675
sections.original: quamne ín manibus ténui atque accépi hic ante aédis
sections.translation: The casket which I took and held in my hands here before the door , where it is I know not ; except , as I fancy , it was dropt by me about this spot . ( Looks about on the ground .) My good sirs ( to the AUDIENCE ) , my kind Spectators , do give me information if any one has seen it , if any one has taken it away , or any one picked it up ; and whether in this direction or that he has taken his departure ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.680
sections.original: non súm scitiór , quae hos rogem aút quae fatígem ,
sections.translation: I ’ m none the wiser for asking these persons , or for worrying them , who are always delighted at a woman ’ s mishaps . Now I ’ ll mark if there are any footsteps here ; for if no one had passed this way since I went in-doors , the casket would be lying here . Why say here ? It ’ s lost , I guess ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.685
sections.original: actum ést , ilicét me infelícem et sceléstam .
sections.translation: it ’ s done for . It ’ s all over with unhappy and unlucky me ! It ’ s nowhere , and nowhere am I . This , by its loss , has proved my loss . But still , as I ’ ve begun , I ’ ll e ’ en go on ; I ’ ll make search ; for both within do I fear , and without I am afraid ; so much , on either side , does fear agitate me now . In this are mortals intensely wretched .

sections.label: Section 4.2.690
sections.original: ílle nunc laétus est , quísquis est , qui íllam habet ,
sections.translation: He is now joyous , whoever he is , that has found it , which is of no use at all to any person else ; to myself it may be . But I cause delay to myself , while I ’ m doing this with remissness . Halisca , attend to what you are about : look down upon the ground , and look round about ; search with your eyes ; guess with shrewdness .

sections.label: Section 4.2.695
sections.original: Era .
sections.translation: Mistress !

sections.label: Section 4.2.695b
sections.original: Hém quid est ?
sections.translation: Well , what ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.695c
sections.original: Haec ést .
sections.translation: That ’ s she .

sections.label: Section 4.2.695d
sections.original: Quis est ?
sections.translation: Who ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.695e
sections.original: Quoi haec éxcidit cistélla .
sections.translation: She who let fall the casket .

sections.label: Section 4.2.696
sections.original: Certe est , eum locum signat , ubí ea excidit : apparet .
sections.translation: Why surely she ’ s tracing out that spot where it fell .

sections.label: Section 4.2.697
sections.original: Sed is hác iit , hac soccí video
sections.translation: But that person has gone this way ; this way I perceive the imprint of his shoe [*] ; this way I ’ ll follow him . ( She moves along , still looking on the ground .) In this spot now has he stopped , along with another person . Here now a circle [*] presents itself to my sight ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.700
sections.original: neque prórsum iit hac : hic stétit , hinc illo
sections.translation: nor did he go straight forward this way ; here he came to a pause . This way did he come out of that circle . Here was a conference with some one . It points to two persons now . Who are these ? Heyday I see the footsteps of only one . But he has gone this way . I ’ ll consider it : hither he went from thence ; from hence he has never gone .

sections.label: Section 4.2.703
sections.original: actám rem ago . quod periít , periit : meum córium cum cistélla .
sections.translation: I ’ m troubling myself to no purpose . What ’ s lost is lost ; my hide [*] along with the casket . I ’ ll go in-doors again .

sections.label: Section 4.2.704b
sections.original: Muliér , mane . sunt quí volunt té conventam .
sections.translation: Hallo , woman-stop ; there are some persons who wish to meet with you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.705
sections.original: Quis mé revocat ?
sections.translation: Who ’ s calling me back ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.705b
sections.original: Bona femina et malus masculus volunt te .
sections.translation: A good female and a bad male want you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.706
sections.original: ⋯ postremo ille
sections.translation: Away with you , bad male ; I want a good one . ( To herself .) After all , he who calls knows better what he wants than I who am called ; I ’ ll return . ( Aloud .) Prithee , have you seen any person hereabouts pick up a casket with some trinkets , which I , to my misfortune , have lost here ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.710
sections.original: nam dudum ut accucurrimus ad Alcésimarchum , ne se
sections.translation: For when , just now , we were running into the house of Alcesimarchus , that he mightn ’ t put an end to his life , at that moment I think that , through terror , the casket fell down from me here .

sections.label: Section 4.2.712
sections.original: Cistellam haec mulier perdidit . tace amus , era , parumper .
sections.translation: This woman ’ s to our purpose ; let ’ s then give heed to her a little , mistress .

sections.label: Section 4.2.713
sections.original: Disperii misera . quid ego erae dicam ? quae me opere tanto
sections.translation: To my sorrow , I ’ m utterly undone . What shall I say to my mistress , who bade me with such earnestness take care of it , through which Silenium might the more readily recognize her parents — who , when little , was adopted by my mistress as her own ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.716
sections.original: quam quaedam meretríx ei dedit .
sections.translation: and whom a certain Courtesan gave to her ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.716b
sections.original: Nostram haec rem fabulatur ,
sections.translation: ( aside .) She ’ s talking about this matter of ours . According as she gives these indications by her talk , she must surely know where your daughter is .

sections.label: Section 4.2.718
sections.original: Nunc eam volt suae matri et patri , quibus nata est , reddere ultro .
sections.translation: Now is she desirous of her own accord to restore her to her father and mother , whose daughter she is ; prithee , my good sir , you are attending to something else ; I commend my matter to you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.720
sections.original: Istuc ago , atque istic mihi cibus est , quod fabulare ,
sections.translation: I ’ m giving my attention to this , and this is as good as food to me , that you are talking of ; but amid my attending to this matter , I was answering this mistress of mine what she was enquiring ; now I return to you . If you have need of anything , say you , and give your orders . What were you looking , for ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.723b
sections.original: Mí homo et mea mulier , vos saluto .
sections.translation: My good sir and my good madam , I greet you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.724
sections.original: Et nos te . sed quid quaeritas ?
sections.translation: And we you . But what are you looking for ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.724b
sections.original: Vestigium hic requiro ,
sections.translation: I ’ m tracing footsteps here ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.725
sections.original: qua aufugit quaedam ⋯ aestio ⋯
sections.translation: the way that something has escaped me here , I don ’ t know how .

sections.label: Section 4.2.725b
sections.original: Quid id ? quid nam est ?
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.726
sections.original: Alienum ⋯ et maerorem familiarem .
sections.translation: Something to bring a loss to another , and a calamity on our family .

sections.label: Section 4.2.727
sections.original: Mala mers , era , haec et callida est .
sections.translation: A worthless baggage is this , mistress , and a crafty one .

sections.label: Section 4.2.727b
sections.original: Ecastor ita videtur .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , and so she seems .

sections.label: Section 4.2.728
sections.original: Imitatur nequam bestiam et damnificam .
sections.translation: She imitates a worthless animal and a mischievous .

sections.label: Section 4.2.728b
sections.original: Quamnam , amabo ?
sections.translation: Which one , prithee ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.729
sections.original: Involvolum , quae in pampini folio intorta implicat se :
sections.translation: A caterpillar , which twisting about winds itself in the leaf of the vine ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.730
sections.original: itidem haec exorditur sibi intortam orationem .
sections.translation: just in the same way does she begin a story that twists about . ( To HALISCA .) What are you looking for ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.731b
sections.original: Cistellula hinc mi , adulescens , evolavit .
sections.translation: A casket , my good young man , has flown away from me here .

sections.label: Section 4.2.732
sections.original: In caveam latam oportuit .
sections.translation: You ought to have put it in a cage .

sections.label: Section 4.2.732b
sections.original: Non edepol praeda magna .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , the booty was no great one .

sections.label: Section 4.2.733
sections.original: Mirum quin grex venalium in cistella infuerit una .
sections.translation: It ’ s a wonder , if a whole troop of slaves [*] isn ’ t there in the casket .

sections.label: Section 4.2.734
sections.original: Sine dicat .
sections.translation: Do let her speak .

sections.label: Section 4.2.734b
sections.original: Si dicat quidem .
sections.translation: If indeed she would speak .

sections.label: Section 4.2.734c
sections.original: Age lóquere tu quid ibi infuerit .
sections.translation: Come say you , what was in it ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.735
sections.original: Crepundia una .
sections.translation: Trinkets only .

sections.label: Section 4.2.735b
sections.original: Est quidam homo , qui illam ait se scire ubi sit .
sections.translation: There ’ s a certain man , who declares that he knows where it is .

sections.label: Section 4.2.736
sections.original: at pol ille a quadam muliere , si eam monstret , gratiam ineat .
sections.translation: But , by my faith , he ’ ll confer an obligation on a certain woman if he ’ ll discover it .

sections.label: Section 4.2.737
sections.original: At sibi ille quidam volt dari mercedem .
sections.translation: But this certain man wishes a reward to be given to him .

sections.label: Section 4.2.737b
sections.original: At pol illa quaedam ,
sections.translation: But , by my faith , this certain woman , that has lost this casket , declares that she has nothing to give to this certain man .

sections.label: Section 4.2.739
sections.original: At enim ille quidam o peram bonam magis expetit quam argentum .
sections.translation: But still this certain man looks for some money .

sections.label: Section 4.2.740
sections.original: At pol illi quoidam mulieri nulla opera gratuita est .
sections.translation: But , by my faith , good woman , in no matter does this certain man give his pains for nothing .

sections.label: Section 4.2.741
sections.original: Commodule quaedam . tu tibi nunc prodes . confitemur
sections.translation: Lend me your conversation : it will now be for your own advantage . We confess that we have got the casket .

sections.label: Section 4.2.742b
sections.original: At vos Salus servassit . ubi ea nunc est ?
sections.translation: Then may Salvation preserve you ; where is it now ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.743
sections.original: Salvam eccam . sed ego rem meam magnam confabulari
sections.translation: See , here it is , safe . But I wish to discourse with you upon a matter of importance to myself ; I take you as a sharer with me in my own preservation .

sections.label: Section 4.2.745
sections.original: Quid istúc negoti est ? aut quis es ?
sections.translation: What matter is this , or who are you ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.745b
sections.original: Egó sum illius mater ,
sections.translation: I am the mother of her who had these things with her , when exposed .

sections.label: Section 4.2.746b
sections.original: Hicine tu ergo habitas ?
sections.translation: Do you live here then ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.746c
sections.original: Hariolare .
sections.translation: You are a diviner . But , prithee , good woman , do lay aside all mystification , and to the point ; tell me at once , whence did you get these trinkets ?
